verdict.answer_first: B states a clearer actionable answer immediately: "start with a Postgres table (via a library, not hand-rolled)"; A is also answer-first but more conditional: "if you already run Postgres... use a DB table."
verdict.decisiveness: B commits harder to one recommendation: "DB table — the right default" and names pg-boss/graphile-worker; A ends by asking for details before committing: "Tell me those and I'll commit to one."
verdict.fabrication: Both use unsupported throughput anchors; A says "thousands of jobs/sec" and B says "under... a few hundred jobs/sec" without evidence.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it gives concrete implementation choices: "Use pg-boss or graphile-worker" plus a quick rule by scenario.
